api/v3/handlers.purpose: Structural umbrella for all v3 HTTP handler sub-packages; each child owns one API resource slice and bridges generated api/v3 request/response types to domain services via the httptransport.Handler[Request,Response] decode/operate/encode pipeline. Every handler here follows the same namespace-resolution, error-encoder, and convert.go conventions.

patterns.name: Type-alias triad per operation file
patterns.description: Each operation file declares Request, Response, and optionally Params type aliases from api/v3 generated types, keeping the file self-contained and searchable.
patterns.example: type ListMetersRequest = api.ListMetersRequestObject; type ListMetersResponse = api.ListMetersResponseObject

patterns.name: NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints; NewHandler for no-param
patterns.description: Endpoints taking URL path parameters (meterID, planID, addonID) must use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs; parameter-less list endpoints use httptransport.NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved in the decoder closure, never the operation closure
patterns.description: Every decoder closure must call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) (or the injected namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace) before building the domain input; namespace must never be extracted in the operation or encoder closures.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return req, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder always last in the error chain
patterns.description: Every handler's AppendOptions block includes httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) so domain model errors map to correct HTTP status; domain-specific encoders chain before it, not instead of it.

patterns.name: convert.go owns mappings; convert.gen.go is Goverter-generated
patterns.description: Hand-written bidirectional conversion lives in convert.go; Goverter generation targets convert.gen.go. Discriminated unions (price types, app types) are always hand-coded. Never edit convert.gen.go directly.

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true on mutating inputs
patterns.description: Create and Update decoders set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true so resources with only non-critical validation issues are not rejected; the flag is set in the decoder, not the domain service.
patterns.example: input.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true // set in decoder closure

patterns.name: Compile-time Handler assertion in handler.go
patterns.description: Every handler.go includes a blank-identifier assertion that the unexported handler struct satisfies the exported Handler interface.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the public Handler interface and unexported handler struct; constructs httptransport.Handler instances wired to injected domain service fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing var _ Handler check; omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder from any AppendOptions; storing a domain service under the wrong interface type.

key_file_guides.role: Owns all bidirectional API<->domain transformations including discriminated unions (price types, app types, source enums).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a union variant in TypeSpec without matching cases; partial round-trips (ToAPI without FromAPI); mixing conversion logic into operation files.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter-generated conversion code. DO NOT EDIT — regenerate with make generate after editing goverter annotations in convert.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any manual edit is silently overwritten on the next make generate.

key_file_guides.role: Present in packages with domain-specific error types (billingprofiles, features); maps domain error structs to HTTP status before GenericErrorEncoder runs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new domain error type without updating this file silently returns 500.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package building streaming.QueryParams from MeterQueryRequest fields; shared by meters and featurecost handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never duplicate this logic inline; always call query.BuildQueryParams. Add reserved dimension keys in query.IsReservedDimension, not in the handler package.

key_file_guides.role: Rich bidirectional transformer for the Plan > Phase > RateCard > Price hierarchy; price-type switch must be exhaustive with an unsupported-type guard (Dynamic/Package return GenericConflictError 409).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a price type in TypeSpec without updating ToAPIBillingPrice, FromAPIBillingPrice, hasUnsupportedV3Price, and convert_test.go round-trip tests.

key_file_guides.role: Wires two subscription services: planSubscriptionService (create/change workflows + billing sync hooks) and subscriptionService (get/list/cancel/continue).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never swap the two services; Create/Change must use the plan service; decode timing as datetime before enum or datetime strings get misread.

key_file_guides.role: Core customer CRUD; sub-packages (billing/, charges/, credits/, entitlementaccess/) own customer-scoped resource slices wired separately.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mixing billing-override/credits/charges operations into the parent package; fetch + IsDeleted() guard before any mutation.

anti_patterns: Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs for any endpoint taking a URL path parameter.
Resolving the namespace inside the operation closure instead of the decoder closure — breaks decode/operate separation and testability.
Hand-editing any convert.gen.go file — it is always overwritten by make generate.
Omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() from an operation's AppendOptions — domain errors return 500 to callers.
Adding business logic (validation, defaults, service calls) inside the encoder (third) closure — all domain calls belong in the operation closure.

decisions.decision: Each resource lives in its own sub-package under api/v3/handlers/ rather than a single flat handlers package.
decisions.rationale: Isolates generated type aliases, conversion logic, and error encoders per resource; prevents import cycles and keeps each package small and independently regenerable.

decisions.decision: Goverter is used only for struct-level field mappings; discriminated unions (price types, app types) are always hand-coded in convert.go.
decisions.rationale: Goverter cannot express type-assertion switches or one-of dispatch; hand-coding makes the exhaustiveness check explicit and reviewable.

decisions.decision: Domain-specific error encoders (billingprofiles, features) chain before GenericErrorEncoder rather than replacing it.
decisions.rationale: GenericErrorEncoder handles common model error types (NotFound, Validation, Conflict); domain encoders only add cases it does not know about.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new path-param v3 handler operation (e.g. GetAddon)
